growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron's March 16-18 updates confirm the AI memory cycle is still tightening, with HBM4 for NVIDIA Vera Rubin already in high-volume production and management expecting DRAM and NAND supply to remain constrained beyond 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron's AI memory upcycle is still tightening: record March 18, 2026 results, HBM4 volume shipments for NVIDIA Vera Rubin, and supply constrained through and beyond 2026 support more upside despite cyclical-capex risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Broadcom remains one of the cleanest second-order AI winners because March 4 results showed AI revenue up 106% to $8.4 billion and guided another step-up to $10.7 billion in Q2 from custom accelerators and AI networking.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Broadcom still looks buyable after March 4, 2026 results showed AI semiconductor revenue up 106% to $8.4B with Q2 AI guidance of $10.7B and a new $10B buyback, but I stop short of STRONG BUY because VMware/software growth is sluggish and insider flow is not supportive.

catalysts_general: Discovery: LLY has the strongest fresh catalyst I found after the FDA approved orforglipron on April 1, 2026, opening a new oral GLP-1 growth leg in Lilly's obesity franchise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fresh FDA approval of Foundayo plus ongoing retatrutide Phase 3 momentum reinforce Lilly's obesity leadership, though pricing pressure keeps it short of a strong-buy setup.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Adobe is a real AI monetizer rather than a concept stock because March 12 results showed AI-first ARR more than tripling year over year, and the March 16 NVIDIA partnership extends Firefly and agentic workflow depth into enterprise production pipelines.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Oversold high-margin software compounder with primary-source-confirmed AI monetization, though CEO transition and competitive AI pressure keep it below STRONG BUY.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The March 5 draft of global AI-chip licensing rules hurt sentiment, but the March 13 reversal preserves Nvidia's rest-of-world accelerator runway and makes the stock a geopolitical policy-relief beneficiary if investors remain anchored on China restrictions alone.
growth_picks.analyst_take: NVIDIA still looks buyable because fiscal Q4 2026 showed 75% Data Center growth with a $78.0B Q1 FY2027 revenue guide excluding China, while Rubin and Blackwell Ultra extend the product cycle into 2H 2026 despite export-control risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square used the latest available 13F season to reveal a new Meta position of 2,673,569 shares worth about $1.76B to $1.80B, and Ackman paired it with explicit commentary that the stock's AI upside was being underappreciated after a valuation reset.
growth_picks.analyst_take: META looks buyable after a 27% drawdown because core ads and AI product monetization are still compounding faster than the current forward multiple implies, with Q1 earnings as the next proof point.
Atlassian looks mispriced after a 71% drawdown because cloud growth, RPO acceleration, and upcoming earnings/product catalysts are still strong enough to outweigh near-term restructuring noise.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CRM is about 37% below its 52-week high while still posting FY2026 net income of $7.46B, and the Feb. 25 authorization of a new $50B repurchase plan followed by the Mar. 16 launch of a record $25B accelerated buyback gives a concrete Q2 support bid; bear case is that debt-funded buybacks do not fully offset AI-disruption fears into the next earnings cycle.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce looks like an oversold quality software compounder with primary-source-backed Agentforce traction and an unusually aggressive $50B repurchase program supporting the rerating case.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Oracle's March 10 quarter remains one of the best proofs that AI cloud demand is monetizing now, with IaaS up 84%, multicloud database revenue up 531%, and RPO hitting $553 billion on large contracted AI workloads.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Oracle is a discounted AI-infrastructure beneficiary with real cloud acceleration and a massive contracted backlog, but extreme leverage and uneven cash flow keep the setup below high-conviction.

catalysts_general: Discovery: ABG qualifies as a meaningful insider-conviction idea after CEO David Hult bought just over $1 million of stock while management still frames 2026 as a transition year with second-half improvement potential.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ABG is a cheap transition-year dealer trading near book value where CEO open-market buying, a late-April earnings catalyst, and a company-guided second-half SG&A/efficiency inflection can re-rate the stock if execution holds.

Accenture looks like a quality services compounder trading at a reset multiple after a federal-spending scare, with record bookings, strong free cash flow, and credible AI execution offsetting still-muted growth.

Blackstone looks like a contrarian quality buy after a roughly 40% drawdown and a 14.6x forward P/E, but conviction stays medium because private-credit redemption pressure is real and recent insider read-through is neutral rather than a fresh catalyst.
Alamos Gold pairs strong margins and a reasonable forward multiple with visible multi-year production growth, but near-term execution and cash flow headwinds keep this below top-tier conviction.

CRDO is a high-growth AI interconnect supplier with strong profitability, cash generation, and fresh optical product catalysts, but the setup is capped by heavy customer concentration, recent dilution, and elevated volatility.
FDA approval of relacorilant in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer creates a real launch-driven reset, but generic Korlym risk and the earlier Cushing's CRL keep this below high-conviction territory.

Mattel screens as a contrarian value buy because the stock is cheap on earnings and free cash flow, the CEO made a meaningful open-market purchase in February, and the May 2026 earnings setup plus buyback authorization provide near-term support despite softer 2026 EPS guidance.
H&R Block is a cheap, cash-generative tax franchise with active buybacks and tax-season AI/product momentum into the May 6, 2026 earnings readout, but regulatory and execution risk cap conviction.

CRSR looks like a contrarian small-cap buyback story with cheap forward valuation, positive FCF, and a creator/sim-racing product cycle, but conviction is capped by management's guided 2026 revenue decline and ongoing semiconductor and tariff exposure.

KKR looks like a contrarian buy after a 40% drawdown because record 2025 fee-related earnings and AUM, the Arctos expansion, and a meaningful February-March insider buying cluster suggest the market is over-discounting private-credit stress.

Oversold quality software franchise: Intuit beat fiscal Q2, reiterated FY2026 guidance, and is accelerating buybacks into a 47.7% drawdown, while spring 2026 AI rollouts offer a credible catalyst for multiple repair.

CROX trades like a broken retailer at roughly 6x forward earnings even though the core Crocs brand is still growing internationally, free cash flow remains strong, and aggressive buybacks give the self-help story time to work while HEYDUDE stabilizes.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PANW has been hit by the software and AI-fear selloff despite staying GAAP-profitable, and CEO Nikesh Arora's roughly $10M open-market buy on Mar. 27 after the sector puke is a high-signal insider catalyst ahead of the confirmed May 26 earnings date; bear case is that multiple compression continues if AI-security spend gets delayed.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Rare CEO buying and strong next-generation security growth support the bull case, but PANW still screens expensive and acquisition-related dilution/integration risk keeps the setup from a fresh BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The White House's March 6 missile-production push and NATO's March 27 estimate that Europe needs roughly $145 billion of extra missile, air-defense, and fires spending tighten the demand backdrop for Patriot, AMRAAM, SM-series, and Stinger supplier RTX.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Record backlog and a multi-year munitions ramp support growth, but RTX is not cheap near highs and still carries Pratt & Whitney GTF and tariff execution risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: A four-person insider cluster hit on March 27, 2026: CEO Anastasios Arima bought about $1.33M, director Todd Hannigan about $498K, President Toby Symonds about $263K, and director R. Tony Tripeny about $99K, signaling coordinated conviction after the stock spent months below its 200-day average.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Government-backed titanium reshoring and a verified four-person insider buying cluster support the story, but IPX remains pre-revenue with commercialization, dilution, and credibility risk after the March short-seller hit.

catalysts_general: Discovery: FCX fits the copper and electrification rotation because the stock is still digesting mine-disruption headlines while the market narrative shifts back toward structural copper deficits and stronger medium-term pricing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Long-life copper optionality and the Grasberg permit extension support the story, but FCX is only modestly below highs and still needs a clean Indonesia restart before the risk/reward justifies a BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: ALB is a contrarian reset candidate because cost cuts and capital discipline are finally showing through while outside analysts have started to argue lithium is near a cyclical bottom.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Albemarle is repairing the business through cost discipline, positive cash generation and portfolio simplification, but the stock has already rebounded hard and still depends heavily on a sustained lithium-price recovery to justify further upside.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Europe's urgent push for long-range fires and air-defense capacity after the March production meetings and NATO spending signal directly supports Lockheed programs such as HIMARS, GMLRS, JASSM/LRASM, and THAAD.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Record backlog and missile-demand tailwinds support the business, but LMT still looks too expensive for a mature defense prime with modest upside and material F-35/program-execution risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Washington is shifting from buyer to builder, with the Pentagon's direct equity investment in L3Harris Missile Solutions and the March 6 production summit highlighting rocket-motor capacity as a strategic bottleneck that L3Harris is paid to relieve.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Missile-demand and portfolio-optimization catalysts are real, but L3Harris is trading close to its highs at a still-rich forward multiple, leaving limited margin of safety for new buying.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Berkshire Hathaway disclosed a new 5,065,744-share New York Times position worth about $351.7M in the February 2026 13F, a meaningful new-media bet from Buffett's shop that the market immediately read as an endorsement of NYT's digital-subscription durability.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality digital-subscription compounder with strong cash generation and no debt draw, but the Berkshire-fueled rerating has left the stock near its high with weak upside versus consensus and clear AI/search disintermediation risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Ziff Davis is an avoid candidate because after agreeing on March 3 to sell the $231 million-revenue Connectivity division, the remaining business is more exposed to AI-overview and zero-click pressure across shopping, reviews, and informational content.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The Accenture deal crystallizes value and can fund debt reduction or buybacks, but post-sale Ziff Davis is more exposed to structurally pressured search- and traffic-driven media assets with no 2026 guidance yet.

catalysts_general: Discovery: GO is down about 68% from its 52-week high and is hated after the Mar. 4 earnings/guidance collapse, but the insider cluster that followed in March around the $6 area is exactly the kind of blood-in-the-streets confirmation a turnaround needs; bear case is that the store refresh and margin fix simply take longer than bulls expect, so this remains a smaller and more speculative setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Heavy March insider buying confirms management sees value near $6, but weak 2026 guidance, 36 store closures and an unproven margin reset keep Grocery Outlet in speculative-turnaround territory rather than buy territory.

catalysts_general: Discovery: TVTX has a clean binary catalyst with FILSPARI's FSGS FDA action date now set for April 13, 2026, making it one of the most immediate biotech event setups on the calendar.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FILSPARI's fast-growing IgAN franchise and the April 13, 2026 FSGS FDA decision keep upside alive, but the missed confirmatory endpoint and FDA's need for extra clinical-benefit support make the pre-decision setup too binary to chase.

catalysts_general: Discovery: HOG is deeply hated but potentially turning under a new CEO, with a fresh strategic plan due in May 2026 and a large ongoing repurchase program that gives the turnaround setup asymmetric upside if volumes stabilize.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap valuation, fresh insider buying, and a May strategy reset create turnaround optionality, but collapsing motorcycle profitability and negative FCF keep Harley-Davidson in prove-it mode.

High-quality aerospace aftermarket compounder with fresh insider buying and acquisition-driven growth, but the stock still screens too expensive relative to earnings and leverage for a disciplined contrarian entry.

Strong free cash flow and management's 2026 growth guide keep Expedia credible, but only moderate upside, explicit Google/AI traffic risk, and no open-market insider-buy signal keep it below buy territory.
Gartner looks optically cheap after a 65% drawdown and aggressive buybacks, but slowing contract value, weak consulting demand, and no meaningful insider buying keep it in wait-for-proof mode.

CNXC is statistically cheap and management is defending the stock with buybacks, debt refinancing, and a fresh CEO open-market buy, but leverage, margin pressure, and AI-disruption risk keep it below a BUY.
Duolingo remains a high-quality, cash-generative learning platform, but the 2026 guidance reset and unresolved AI/distribution risks keep the post-crash valuation from being a clean buy.
Elite underwriting and an active buyback support KNSL, but commercial-property deceleration and only moderate upside keep the risk/reward balanced ahead of Q1 results.

PayPal is statistically cheap with heavy buyback support and credible AI-commerce partnerships, but the branded-checkout slowdown, lower engagement, and February 2026 CEO reset keep the turnaround unproven.

Apple remains a high-quality cash machine, but at roughly 32x trailing earnings with the stock only about 11% below its 52-week high, near-term upside looks limited until April 30 earnings and WWDC26 prove the AI story can reaccelerate.
Meta and NVIDIA validate Nebius as a real AI-infrastructure platform, but the stock remains an execution-heavy, cash-burning buildout story after another large convert raise.
Snap is cheap enough to stay on the radar, but the profitable-growth pivot still competes with regulatory overhang, unresolved AI rollout questions, and insider selling rather than insider buying.

SOFI is posting real growth and fee-based diversification, but the March 17 accounting-focused short attack leaves too much unresolved balance-sheet and LPB risk to chase before the April 29 earnings catalyst.

AI-photonics commercialization is finally moving toward 2026 shipments and sample milestones, but POET still has sub-$1M quarterly revenue, negative operating cash flow, and recent insider selling, so the setup is too speculative to buy aggressively yet.

AppLovin still looks fundamentally elite on growth, margins, and cash flow, but the active SEC probe and net insider selling keep the risk/reward from being clean enough to buy aggressively before the next print.
Order flow and new defense programs are real, but AVAV still looks too expensive and execution-heavy after the SCAR termination, negative FCF, and ongoing integration work from BlueHalo and ESAero.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Iran-war disruption around Hormuz and Qatar's halted LNG output are pushing buyers toward U.S. replacement cargoes, making Cheniere the cleanest large-cap way to monetize emergency non-Gulf LNG demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheniere remains a high-quality LNG cash flow compounder with visible 2026 volume growth, but the stock is already near its highs and the current setup offers limited incremental upside versus risk.
Cheap on forward earnings and backed by near-term execution catalysts, but revenue-quality questions, receivables concentration, and a fresh short-report overhang keep ADMA in prove-it mode.

Coupang still has a credible recovery setup through Q1 normalization, Taiwan hypergrowth, and notable insider buying, but the stock is not cheap enough yet for a clean BUY given breach, regulatory, and margin risks.
Robotaxi and Optimus optionality do not offset shrinking auto fundamentals, a soft Q1 delivery setup, and an extreme valuation multiple.

Interlink has an interesting sensor narrative, but recent SEC filings still show a subscale company with negative margins, minimal institutional sponsorship, and no verified near-term catalyst strong enough to justify bottom-fishing after a 69% drawdown.

Spectrum-sale proceeds could eventually stabilize EchoStar, but the March 2026 10-K still carries a going-concern warning, weak liquidity, and material execution risk around the rescue transactions, leaving SATS as a restructuring trade rather than an investable equity.

catalysts_general: Discovery: With Plaquemines ramping as Washington spotlights Louisiana LNG in its March 10 industrial-policy push and Asian buyers scramble for non-Qatar cargoes, Venture Global has unusually direct exposure to both Gulf Coast buildout and emergency replacement demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Plaquemines ramp-up and new LNG contracts are real catalysts, but uncapped BP arbitration exposure, very heavy leverage/negative FCF, and recent insider selling leave too little margin of safety.
Nike is cheaper than it was but still not cheap enough for a turnaround with flat sales, falling margins, weak China, and benefits pushed into FY2027.
